TranslationContext: This file contains Unit Names for the Knights of Avalon Faction.

Translations.Comment: Noble knights. Direct translation emphasizing legendary kingdom heritage.
Sword troops. 'Spadaccino' conveys skilled sword-wielder, giving martial feel.
Untrained levies. Emphasizes disorganized masses of village folk.
Halberd troops. 'Alabardiera' denotes those who wield halberds professionally.
Spear troops. 'Lanciera' denotes lance/spear-armed basic infantry.
Training knights. Clear designation of young warriors on path to knighthood.
Light cavalry. Emphasizes mounted reconnaissance and hunting role.
Oath-bound knights. 'Giurati' conveys solemnity of sacred vows and duty.
War dogs. Simple, direct designation of hunting/combat beasts.
Scout archers. Versatile ranged combatants and reconnaissance troops.
Siege weapon. Italian adaptation maintains technical precision in wargame context.
Elite archers. 'Arcieri lunghi' emphasizes specialized ranged prowess.
Royal elite. Standard term emphasizing service to crown and elite status.
Flying cavalry. Maintains fantasy aesthetic while identifying sky-riders.
Fantasy hybrid creature. Standard Italian term for gryphon-horse hybrid.
Winged horse. Standard Italian adaptation of mythological name.
Magical beast. Standard Italian term for unicorn.
Holy warrior maiden. 'Donzella' carries noble and sacred connotations for divine warrior.
Holy knight. Direct Italian adaptation of universal fantasy term.
